quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 10 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 10 Quiz 11: Statistics & Data Analysis — Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 10: Statistics & Data Analysis — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: A student's grades: Homework (20% weight, average 92), Quizzes (30% weight, average 78), Tests (50% weight, average 85). Find the weighted mean.
Find the population standard deviation of: $3, 7, 7, 11$. Round to two decimal places.
A normal distribution has $\mu = 65$ and $\sigma = 7$. What percentage of data falls between 51 and 79?
questions.explanation: 51 = $65 - 2(7) = \mu - 2\sigma$. 79 = $65 + 2(7) = \mu + 2\sigma$. By the Empirical Rule, 95% falls within $\pm 2\sigma$.

questions.question: A normal distribution has $\mu = 100$ and $\sigma = 15$. What percentage of data falls between 85 and 115?
questions.explanation: 85 = $\mu - 1\sigma$, 115 = $\mu + 1\sigma$. By the Empirical Rule, 68% falls within $\pm 1\sigma$.

questions.question: A data set has $\mu = 55$ and $\sigma = 8$. Find the $z$-score for $x = 43$.
questions.explanation: $z = \frac{x - \mu}{\sigma} = \frac{43 - 55}{8} = \frac{-12}{8} = -1.5$. The value is 1.5 standard deviations below the mean.

questions.question: A normal distribution has $\mu = 72$ and $\sigma = 6$. What data value corresponds to $z = 2.25$?
A sample of 400 people finds 240 support a proposition. Find the sample proportion and the 95% confidence interval using $MOE = 1/\sqrt{n}$.
A poll wants a margin of error of 3%. What sample size is needed? Use $MOE = 1/\sqrt{n}$. Round up.
A regression line is $\hat{y} = 2.5x - 10$. For $x = 20$, the actual value is $y = 42$. Find the predicted value and the residual.
A regression line $\hat{y} = 3x + 1$ is fit to points $(1, 3)$, $(2, 8)$, $(3, 9)$. Find the sum of squared residuals.
questions.explanation: Residuals: $3-4=-1$, $8-7=1$, $9-10=-1$. Squared: $1+1+1 = 3$. This measures total prediction error.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 10: weighted mean, standard deviation, Empirical Rule, z-scores, confidence intervals, margin of error with sample size, regression prediction, and residuals.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) Weighted mean = sum of (weight × value), (2) $\sigma = \sqrt{\frac{\sum(x-\bar{x})^2}{n}}$ for population, (3) Empirical Rule: 68-95-99.7, (4) $z = \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}$ and $x = \mu + z\sigma$.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Computing deviations and squaring them for standard deviation, (2) Using the Empirical Rule for symmetric and asymmetric intervals, (3) $MOE = 1/\sqrt{n}$ and solving for $n$, (4) Residual = actual − predicted.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 10 fundamentals: (1) Mean = sum/n; weighted mean = sum of (weight × value), (2) $\sigma = \sqrt{\frac{\sum(x-\bar{x})^2}{n}}$, (3) Empirical Rule: 68% within $\pm1\sigma$, 95% within $\pm2\sigma$, 99.7% within $\pm3\sigma$, (4) $z = \frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}$, (5) $MOE = 1/\sqrt{n}$, CI = statistic $\pm$ MOE, (6) Residual = $y - \hat{y}$.
